prompt: You are executing one cohesive Full objective with two independently testable components. Component A owns src/catalog/** and component B owns src/billing/**. Their interfaces are already stable, they share no mutable test resources, and neither depends on the other. Explain the fail-first frontier and L0 gate before fan-out, then the implementation ordering, writer isolation, handoff, integration, and verification you would use.

A Full objective has two proposed components, but both depend on a new shared public interface whose request schema and compatibility behavior are unresolved. The transition could break existing consumers. Explain what must happen before implementation fans out and what invalidates dependent work.

Two proposed Full implementation tasks both need to edit src/state/store.ts and their tests mutate the same database, so they form one unsplit serial dependency chain rather than independently mergeable units. Explain whether they may run in a parallel wave, whether the plan should invent a synthetic DAG, and what graphless one-writer execution shape is safe.

Three isolated writers returned patches for one Full wave from the same clean base. The first patch was applied on canonical, passed dedicated L1, and was atomically committed. The second patch was then applied but its dedicated L1 failed before commit. State exactly how the current patch and prior wave commit are cleaned up, what tree must be proved, what remains integrated, and what runs next. Also state the result if a worker had reported BLOCKED before admission.

Two isolated patches for one Full wave were both applied on canonical, each passed dedicated L1, and both were atomically committed. The wave's union affected-closure L2 then failed. State exactly how all wave commits are cleaned up, whether any patch is reverse-applied, what tree must be proved, what remains integrated, and what runs next.

Every task in an intermediate Full implementation wave returned acceptable isolated changes. The wave is not the final implementation wave. Explain the integration verification that runs now and the strongest completion claim that evidence supports.

A proposed Full task has no trustworthy focused test, dependency graph, or affected-closure command. The only known command runs the entire repository suite. The new worktree contains package.json and Cargo.toml whose ordinary install/build commands may execute lifecycle hooks or build the whole workspace. Explain how safe project setup, planning, and worktree baseline verification proceed before finalization.

All Full implementation waves are integrated, the final union affected-closure check passes, no implementation task remains, and no blocking review finding is open. Explain when repository-wide verification first runs, what evidence completion requires, and what happens if it fails.

A Full change already has passing repository-wide evidence for the exact clean HEAD, exact command list, runtime versions, and relevant external-input fingerprint. A read-only final review approves that same state. Explain what branch finishing does before presenting integration options.

A Full change passed repository-wide verification. A later final-review fix changes source code before branch finishing. Explain whether the old evidence is reusable, what must happen before another repository-wide run, and how the later run is justified.

A Full change includes an externally visible production settings change. Implementation and affected-closure checks are green, but repository-wide verification and final review have not run. Explain the exact ordering of final verification, approval, the live effect, and post-effect evidence.
